name: Swiss Cheese Fondue
recipeInstructions:
1. Toss the cheeses with cornstarch until coated.
2. Rub garlic on the bottom and side of a heavy saucepan or skillet.
3. Add wine to the pan and heat over low heat just until bubbles rise to the surface (wine should not boil).
4. Stir in lemon juice.
5. Gradually add cheeses, about 1/2 cup at a time, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon over low heat until the cheeses are melted.
6. Stir in Kirsch, salt, and white pepper.
7. Remove the mixture to an earthenware fondue dish and keep warm over low heat.
8. Spear bread cubes with fondue forks and dip and swirl in fondue using a stirring motion. If the fondue becomes too thick, stir in 1/4 to 1/2 cup heated wine. An additional 2 cups shredded Swiss cheese can be substituted for Gruyere cheese.
